model.label: GPT-5.4
model.description: OpenAI • Frontier GPT model for general-purpose reasoning, tool use, and agentic workflows.

pick.rationale: Houston gets the slight edge because this is essentially a top-tier heavyweight matchup, but the Cougars are a bit more balanced overall and bring the clearly better defense into a game against Illinois’ elite offense. Illinois absolutely has a credible path if its No. 1-caliber attack wins the shot-making battle and forces Houston out of its comfort zone, so confidence stays modest. With no major availability issue clearly shifting the matchup, Houston’s stronger defensive floor and nearly equal overall rating make it the safer Sweet 16 pick.

reasoningStep.title: Balanced Houston over explosive Illinois
reasoningStep.summary: Ratings show a very small overall gap, with Houston No. 5 in both KenPom and Torvik and Illinois No. 6-7, but Houston’s defense is materially stronger while Illinois owns the better offense. No clear injury news surfaced to materially change the baseline, so the decision comes down to whether Houston’s defensive consistency is more trustworthy than Illinois’ offensive ceiling.
evidence: Houston ratings: KenPom No. 5 (AdjEM 33.55, AdjO 124.6 No. 16, AdjD 91.0), Torvik No. 5 (AdjO 125.10 No. 13, AdjD 91.85 No. 4).
Illinois ratings: KenPom No. 7 (AdjEM 32.14, AdjO 131.2 No. 1, AdjD 99.1), Torvik No. 6 (AdjO 131.96 No. 2, AdjD 98.14 No. 24).
Web and ESPN news searches did not surface a confirmed major availability absence for either team significant enough to override the statistical matchup.
Underdog path considered: Illinois can absolutely win by turning this into an offense-first game, but Houston’s elite defense gives it the steadier profile in a close Sweet 16 call.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
